title: Agent session state (+ manager heartbeat)
description: Top-level schema = the durable session-state file the manager persists across restarts (today: ~/.claude/shared/agent-session-state.json, mode 0600, written atomically). The heartbeat status snapshot the manager rewrites every supervisor tick lives under $defs/heartbeat; the single-owner channel-lock state under $defs/channelLock. Field `sessionId` is deliberately camelCase for drop-in rollback compatibility with the legacy manager.js file.

schema_version.description: Contract version. Legacy files (manager.js / manager-rs v0) omit it; consumers MUST treat absence as version 1. Producers MUST write it.

sessionId.description: The Claude Code session id this agent resumes. camelCase on disk on purpose (manager.js rollback compatibility).

bootstrapped.description: false = freshly minted id, launch with --session-id <id>; true = previously spawned, launch with --resume <id>. Absent (legacy manager.js file) MUST default to true: that session has already run, so resume is correct.

heartbeat.description: Status snapshot rewritten every supervisor tick (1s). Consumed by the `healthcheck` subcommand and the canary deploy driver: heartbeat fresh (default max 30s), pid alive, state allowed, pane alive+tagged when running, Matrix sync fresh (default max 120s). Epoch-seconds timestamps are retained from the deployed v1 shape (healthcheck freshness math is epoch arithmetic); 0 means 'never'.

schema_version.description: Contract version. The live manager-rs writes this integer under the key `schema` with value 1; version 2 IS the rename to the fleet-standard `schema_version` key. Consumers during migration: accept `schema:1` as the v1 equivalent.

version.description: Manager build version (today: cargo package version).

handle.description: Fleet handle of the agent this manager supervises (e.g. 'janet'). New in v2: lets one host run several managers without ambiguity.

pid.description: Manager process id (valid on Windows too).

session_id.description: The supervised Claude Code session id (mirrors sessionState.sessionId).

tmux_session.description: tmux session holding the agent pane. null on platforms without tmux (Windows box agents) — contracts are OS-neutral; consumers MUST NOT require it.

pane_id.description: Owned (tagged) tmux pane id, e.g. '%12'. null when no live pane, or on non-tmux platforms.

io_ok.description: false = we own/monitor a pane but won't inject keys into it.

started_at_epoch.description: Epoch seconds the agent was spawned/adopted; 0 = never.

last_sync_ok_epoch.description: Epoch seconds of the last successful Matrix /sync; 0 = never (healthcheck fails on 0).

updated_at_epoch.description: Epoch seconds this snapshot was written; freshness gate for healthcheck.

channel_lock.description: Single-owner Matrix channel lock state (new in v2; LOCKED requirement — the lock is load-bearing).

channelLock.description: Expresses the single-owner Matrix channel lock (matrix-channel-rs). Exactly one process may own the channel (send/receive as the agent). 'lockout' = this process detected another owner and stood down (e.g. the Focus-squat reboot race, 2026-07-09): it MUST NOT send on the channel until the lock is re-acquired.

state.description: held = we own the channel; released = we cleanly gave it up; lockout = another owner detected, we stood down.

owner.description: Identity currently believed to hold the channel (device/session identifier). null when unknown.

acquired_at_epoch.description: Epoch seconds we (or the observed owner) took the lock; 0 = unknown.

contender.description: When state=lockout: the identity we observed squatting the channel, for diagnostics.
